---

# UC-18.2.16 · Edge Cluster BFD and Uplink Session Health

## Description

Correlates **VMware NSX-T** **Tier-0** **edge** transport-node **BFD** neighbor telemetry (**`vmware:nsx:bfd_session`**) with **uplink** posture rows (**`vmware:nsx:edge_status`**), then enriches with **transport-node** **maintenance** hints (**`vmware:nsx:transport_node`**), **Policy**/**Manager** **audit** touches (**`vmware:nsx:audit`**), and **vCenter** host-motion context (**`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`**) so teams see **sub-second** hello loss, **LACP**-visible **uplink** regressions, and **inter-site** stretch instability before **north-south** paths go **asymmetric** while **BGP** still appears **Established**.

## Value

Customers feel **brownout** **routing** long before **ticketing** closes: **one** warm **uplink** carries **traffic** while **BFD** on the **cold** leg is **DOWN**, **HA** **SR** roles look healthy, and **applications** **flap** on **retries**. Giving **edge**, **carrier**, and **platform** owners a single **Splunk** **story**—**which** **edge** **node**, **which** **Tier-0**, **which** **peer** **IP**, **which** **physical** **uplink**, with **audit**/**vCenter** **context**—shortens **bridge** calls, stops **finger-pointing** between **VMware** and **WAN** teams, and funds **targeted** **failover** drills instead of **mystery** **packet** **loss** **war** rooms.

## Implementation

Enable **HTTPS** collectors (or **TA** modular inputs) for **BFD** neighbors and **inter-site**/**status** routes; normalize **`bfd_state`**, **`uplink_status`**, **`edge_node_id`**, **`tier0_id`**; ship **transport-node** inventory and **audit** feeds; map **Edge** **VM** **MoIDs** into **`vcenter_host_map.csv`** for **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** joins; deploy **`uc_18_2_16_edge_bfd_uplink`** on a **5m** cadence with **`nsx_planned_edge_window.csv`** throttling; publish **`ds_nsx_edge_bfd_uplink`**.

## Evidence

Saved search **`uc_18_2_16_edge_bfd_uplink`**, optional **Dashboard Studio** workbook **`ds_nsx_edge_bfd_uplink`** with **`tok_edge_cluster`**, **`tok_tier0`**, **`tok_uplink`** tokens, plus weekly **CSV** exports of **`bfd_state_latest`** from **`vmware:nsx:bfd_session`** joined to **NSX Manager** screenshots.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**
• **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) wherever `vmware:nsx:bfd_session`, `vmware:nsx:edge_status`, `vmware:nsx:transport_node`, and `vmware:nsx:audit` parsing runs for VMware NSX 4.2 / 4.1 / NSX-T 3.2.x per Broadcom lifecycle pages.
• **vCenter context** — Add **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) so `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` captures maintenance, vMotion, and evacuation tasks tied to Edge VM MoIDs (map MoID↔`edge_node_id` with `vcenter_host_map.csv`).
• **Syslog scale** — Terminate high-volume Edge `/var/log` feeds on **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) with precise `TIME_PREFIX` for ISO-8601 timestamps.
• **ACI hybrid (optional)** — Install **`Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) when Tier-0 handoffs land on ACI L3Out SVIs; use `cisco:aci:syslog` as corroboration, not a substitute NSX signal. Sample fabric dashboards may reference **Cisco Data Center Networking** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)).
• **RBAC & TLS** — Collector accounts remain read-only for transport-node status, logical-router BFD JSON, Policy `.../tier-0s/{id}/locale-services/.../bgp/neighbors/status`, and audit categories; avoid TLS MITM on Manager/Policy 443 that breaks client certificates; honor HTTP 429 with jittered backoff.
• **Lookup hygiene** — Maintain `vcenter_host_map.csv` and `nsx_planned_edge_window.csv` (`tier0_path`, `start_epoch`, `end_epoch`, `chg_id`) before any paging macros fire.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) **`vmware:nsx:bfd_session`** — HTTPS workers every 60–120s call `GET /api/v1/logical-routers/{logical-router-id}/routing/bfd/neighbors` (resolve `{logical-router-id}` from Tier-0 SR inventory); emit `bfd_state`, `peer_ip`, discriminators, `edge_node_id`, `tier0_id`.
(2) **`vmware:nsx:edge_status`** — Poll `GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/inter-site/status` and companion Edge status JSON your operations guide names; capture `uplink_status`, `uplink_name`, `message`, `edge_node_id`.
(3) **`vmware:nsx:transport_node`** — Scheduled `GET /api/v1/transport-nodes` (or standardized Policy mirrors) with `admin_state`, `maintenance_mode`, `cluster_id`, `node_type` for maintenance joins.
(4) **`vmware:nsx:audit`** — Ship audit/API trails when `request_uri` touches `tier-0`, `locale-service`, `bfd`, `edge-cluster`, or transport-node relocations.
(5) **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** — Include tasks or events that show ESXi maintenance when Edge VMs evacuate; enrich with `vcenter_host_map.csv` so join keys align with NSX IDs.
(6) **Volume math** — Budget ≈5–15 KB/min per active Edge when BFD plus inter-site polls run; isolate hot BFD indexes from verbose syslog archives.

**Step 2 — Create the search**
Promote the SPL into macro **`uc_18_2_16_edge_bfd_uplink`** when index aliases stabilize, then iterate thresholds inside `macros.conf` rather than inline.

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.16 Tier-0 edge BFD + uplink; five sourcetypes via joins")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) (sourcetype="vmware:nsx:bfd_session" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(edge_node_id,transport_node_id,edge_node,"")))
| eval tier0_key=trim(lower(coalesce(tier0_id,logical_router_id,"")))
| eval peer_key=trim(coalesce(bfd_peer_ip,peer_ip,bgp_neighbor_ip,""))
| eval uplink_key=trim(coalesce(uplink_name,uplink_id,pnic_name,""))
| eval bfd_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(bfd_state,bfd_session_state,"")))
| eval uplink_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(uplink_status,link_status,"")))
| eval diag_txt=lower(coalesce(message,detail,_raw,""))
| eval bfd_fault=if(bfd_raw IN ("DOWN","ADMIN_DOWN","ADMINDOWN","INIT"),1,0)
| eval uplink_fault=if(match(uplink_raw,"^(DOWN|FAILED|DEGRADED|DISABLED)$") OR match(diag_txt,"(?i)uplink.+(down|loss|flap)"),1,0)
| eval syslog_bfd=if(sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status" AND match(diag_txt,"(?i)bfd.+(down|timeout|expir|hold)"),1,0)
| eval fault_class=case(bfd_fault==1 AND sourcetype="vmware:nsx:bfd_session","bfd_neighbor_not_up",uplink_fault==1,"uplink_not_healthy",syslog_bfd==1,"bfd_story_in_edge_status",null())
| where isnotnull(fault_class)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(bfd_raw) as bfd_state_latest latest(uplink_raw) as uplink_state_latest latest(peer_key) as peer_latest latest(uplink_key) as uplink_latest latest(tier0_key) as tier0_latest max(_time) as last_evt by edge_key fault_class
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:transport_node" earliest=-6h@m latest=now
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(id,uuid,transport_node_id,"")))
| eval mm=if(match(lower(coalesce(admin_state,maintenance_mode,_raw)),"(?i)(mainten|disabled|quarantine)"),1,0)
| stats max(mm) as tn_in_maintenance values(cluster_name) as tn_cluster values(node_type) as tn_role by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:audit" earliest=-6h@m latest=now
| where match(lower(coalesce(request_uri,resource_path,_raw)),"(?i)tier-0|bfd|locale-service|edge-cluster|transport-node")
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(target_transport_node,resource_id,source_id,"")))
| stats latest(_time) as audit_touch latest(user_name) as audit_actor by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:vcenter" earliest=-6h@m latest=now
| where match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance|evacuat|vmotion")
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(host_id,host_mo_id,"")))
| stats latest(_time) as vc_touch max(if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance"),1,0)) as vc_mm_hint by edge_key ]
| eval severity=case(fault_class="bfd_neighbor_not_up",90,fault_class="uplink_not_healthy",85,fault_class="bfd_story_in_edge_status",75,1==1,50)
| eval story=edge_key." | ".fault_class." | ".coalesce(tier0_latest,"no-tier0")
| eval suppress_candidate=if(tn_in_maintenance==1 OR vc_mm_hint==1 OR audit_touch>relative_time(last_evt,"-10m"),1,0)
| where suppress_candidate==0
| sort - severity - last_evt
| head 400
| table last_evt severity edge_key tier0_latest uplink_latest peer_latest bfd_state_latest uplink_state_latest fault_class tn_cluster tn_role audit_touch audit_actor vc_touch story
```

**Understanding this SPL**
• **Fault classes** — `bfd_neighbor_not_up` surfaces non-UP `bfd_state` rows; `uplink_not_healthy` catches failed uplink enums or uplink-down language in `message`; `bfd_story_in_edge_status` ingests BFD verbs embedded in status payloads when the dedicated BFD feed lags.
• **Deduplication** — `bin` + `stats` collapse five-minute bursts so flapping optics do not spam results; tune span per poll cadence.
• **Maintenance enrichment** — `transport_node` join stamps `tn_in_maintenance`; `vmware:nsx:audit` shows who published changes minutes before symptoms; `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` hints host MM (MoID joins may require your CSV glue—verify joins before paging).
• **Suppression logic** — `suppress_candidate` drops rows when transport nodes maintenance, vCenter MM hints, or audit touches land within ten minutes of `last_evt`; adjust the lag to match your change discipline.
• **Severity** — BFD loss sorts above pure uplink labels so carrier + VMware bridges prioritize hello truth ahead of softer syslog narratives.

**Step 3 — Validate**
(1) **UI parity** — NSX Manager Tier-0 + Edge views should show identical `peer_ip` / `bfd_state` pairs as `peer_latest` / `bfd_state_latest` for the same `edge_key`.
(2) **Underlay** — Compare `uplink_latest` to switch `show lacp neighbor` / `show interface` on both ToRs; mismatches here belong to physical teams first.
(3) **Stretch** — If inter-site labels appear, validate MTU/RTT with Path Health tooling (UC-18.2.9) before declaring this UC a dead end.
(4) **Audit pairing** — When `fault_class` spikes, locate matching `vmware:nsx:audit` rows referencing locale-service edits or BFD-adjacent URIs.
(5) **Cardinality guard** — `dc(edge_key)` for fault rows should stay near your installed physical Edge count; multiples imply duplicate pollers or stale IDs.
(6) **Join truth** — If `vc_touch` never populates, refresh `vcenter_host_map.csv`; blank MoID joins are expected until the CSV lands.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
(1) **Schedule** — Saved search every five minutes writing to summary index `summary_nsx_edge_bfd` (optional) for leadership rollups.
(2) **Ticketing** — Push `story`, `tier0_latest`, `peer_latest`, and `uplink_latest` into ServiceNow custom fields; attach NSX UI deep links from `lookup_nsx_ui.csv`.
(3) **Data quality** — Alert when `vmware:nsx:bfd_session` goes silent >15m for production clusters—stale happy data hurts more than noisy polls.
(4) **Ownership** — Restrict macro edits to `splunk_role_nsx_edge`; WAN teams get filtered dashboards with `peer_latest` tokens only.
(5) **ES correlation** — Optional Enterprise Security notables may wrap CIM `Alerts`; keep raw observability panels for engineering drill-down.

**Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot**
**Visualization guidance** — Dashboard Studio `ds_nsx_edge_bfd_uplink`: Row A single-values for counts of `bfd_neighbor_not_up`, `uplink_not_healthy`, `bfd_story_in_edge_status` with tokens `tok_edge_cluster`, `tok_tier0`, `tok_uplink`. Row B matrix of `edge_key` × `uplink_latest` colored by `severity` with tooltips showing `peer_latest`. Row C timeline stacking `fault_class` by `_time`. Row D detail table for `audit_actor`, `audit_touch`, and `tn_cluster` so NetOps sees who touched Policy during incidents. Optional choropleth when `tier0_latest` maps to campus or metro site codes for NOC video walls.
**Alert design** — `ALRT_NSX_BFD_HARD`: `fault_class=bfd_neighbor_not_up`, `severity>=90`, throttle 15m per `edge_key`+`peer_latest`, require two polls. `ALRT_NSX_UPLINK_SOFT`: `uplink_not_healthy`, throttle 30m per `uplink_latest`, suppress when `tn_in_maintenance=1` or `inputlookup nsx_planned_edge_window` matches `tier0_latest`. Notables must carry `bfd_state_latest`, `uplink_state_latest`, `chg_id`, and deep links; route WAN optics issues using `lacp_sync_renegotiation_noise` runbook language.
**Troubleshooting** — BGP may remain Established while BFD is down—escalate WAN/MTU with traceroute evidence. Manager VIP rolling upgrades may stale `edge_status` JSON (`inter_site_controller_degraded_poll`). Collector pagination gaps yield blank `peer_latest` (`api_cursor_partial_page`). Service restarts after Edge patches can flash INIT (`edge_service_restart_blip`). GR windows may hold BFD in INIT (`bfd_hold_gr_convergence`). Labs with single uplinks trip `single_attached_uplink_test_mode`—filter non-prod edge clusters.
**Governance** — Quarterly compare Splunk `fault_class` histograms to tabletop CRQ narratives; log collector journal gaps in postmortems.
**Capacity** — If search finalizes early, summarize ten-minute `edge_key`×`fault_class` buckets into `summary_nsx_edge_bfd`.
**Training** — Rehearse reading Dashboard Studio first, CLI `get bfd neighbors` second, carrier portals third.

**Instrumentation checklist** — After TA upgrades run `fieldsummary` on `vmware:nsx:bfd_session` for **`bfd_state`**, **`peer_ip`**, **`edge_node_id`**, **`tier0_id`**; confirm `vmware:nsx:edge_status` still extracts **`uplink_status`**, **`uplink_name`**, **`message`**; verify `vmware:nsx:transport_node` exposes **`admin_state`**, **`maintenance_mode`**; ensure audit retains **`request_uri`**, **`user_name`**; validate vCenter `_time` skew <1 poll interval; monitor derived fields **`last_evt`**, **`severity`**, **`edge_key`**, **`tier0_latest`**, **`uplink_latest`**, **`peer_latest`**, **`bfd_state_latest`**, **`uplink_state_latest`**, **`fault_class`**, **`tn_cluster`**, **`tn_role`**, **`audit_touch`**, **`audit_actor`**, **`vc_touch`**, **`story`**, **`suppress_candidate`**, **`tn_in_maintenance`**, **`vc_mm_hint`**, **`bfd_fault`**, **`uplink_fault`**, **`syslog_bfd`**, **`diag_txt`**, **`bfd_raw`**, **`uplink_raw`**, **`peer_key`**, **`uplink_key`**, **`tier0_key`** inside saved-search unit tests.

**Glossary** — **BFD** means subsecond forward fault detection. **Locale service** is the Tier-0 dataplane attachment on Edge clusters. **Inter-site** names stretched transport-node composites. **Edge cluster** references active/standby or active-active SR groupings. **MoID** is the vCenter managed object id tying hosts to NSX ids.

**Sibling handoff** — Pair with **UC-18.2.4** for BGP state economics and **UC-18.2.12** for SR HA failover rhythm before blaming uplinks alone; escalate GENEVE overlays through **UC-18.2.9** when pathology is tunnel—not BFD—related.

**Operator cadence** — **Monthly** screenshots from `ds_nsx_edge_bfd_uplink`; **Quarterly** TA diff review; **Annual** WAN circuit lookup `lookup_wan_circuit.csv` refresh with carriers.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.16 Tier-0 edge BFD + uplink; five sourcetypes via joins")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) (sourcetype="vmware:nsx:bfd_session" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(edge_node_id,transport_node_id,edge_node,"")))
| eval tier0_key=trim(lower(coalesce(tier0_id,logical_router_id,"")))
| eval peer_key=trim(coalesce(bfd_peer_ip,peer_ip,bgp_neighbor_ip,""))
| eval uplink_key=trim(coalesce(uplink_name,uplink_id,pnic_name,""))
| eval bfd_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(bfd_state,bfd_session_state,"")))
| eval uplink_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(uplink_status,link_status,"")))
| eval diag_txt=lower(coalesce(message,detail,_raw,""))
| eval bfd_fault=if(bfd_raw IN ("DOWN","ADMIN_DOWN","ADMINDOWN","INIT"),1,0)
| eval uplink_fault=if(match(uplink_raw,"^(DOWN|FAILED|DEGRADED|DISABLED)$") OR match(diag_txt,"(?i)uplink.+(down|loss|flap)"),1,0)
| eval syslog_bfd=if(sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status" AND match(diag_txt,"(?i)bfd.+(down|timeout|expir|hold)"),1,0)
| eval fault_class=case(bfd_fault==1 AND sourcetype="vmware:nsx:bfd_session","bfd_neighbor_not_up",uplink_fault==1,"uplink_not_healthy",syslog_bfd==1,"bfd_story_in_edge_status",null())
| where isnotnull(fault_class)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(bfd_raw) as bfd_state_latest latest(uplink_raw) as uplink_state_latest latest(peer_key) as peer_latest latest(uplink_key) as uplink_latest latest(tier0_key) as tier0_latest max(_time) as last_evt by edge_key fault_class
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:transport_node" earliest=-6h@m latest=now
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(id,uuid,transport_node_id,"")))
| eval mm=if(match(lower(coalesce(admin_state,maintenance_mode,_raw)),"(?i)(mainten|disabled|quarantine)"),1,0)
| stats max(mm) as tn_in_maintenance values(cluster_name) as tn_cluster values(node_type) as tn_role by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:audit" earliest=-6h@m latest=now
| where match(lower(coalesce(request_uri,resource_path,_raw)),"(?i)tier-0|bfd|locale-service|edge-cluster|transport-node")
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(target_transport_node,resource_id,source_id,"")))
| stats latest(_time) as audit_touch latest(user_name) as audit_actor by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:vcenter" earliest=-6h@m latest=now
| where match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance|evacuat|vmotion")
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(host_id,host_mo_id,"")))
| stats latest(_time) as vc_touch max(if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance"),1,0)) as vc_mm_hint by edge_key ]
| eval severity=case(fault_class="bfd_neighbor_not_up",90,fault_class="uplink_not_healthy",85,fault_class="bfd_story_in_edge_status",75,1==1,50)
| eval story=edge_key." | ".fault_class." | ".coalesce(tier0_latest,"no-tier0")
| eval suppress_candidate=if(tn_in_maintenance==1 OR vc_mm_hint==1 OR audit_touch>relative_time(last_evt,"-10m"),1,0)
| where suppress_candidate==0
| sort - severity - last_evt
| head 400
| table last_evt severity edge_key tier0_latest uplink_latest peer_latest bfd_state_latest uplink_state_latest fault_class tn_cluster tn_role audit_touch audit_actor vc_touch story
```

## Visualization

(1) **Matrix** — **`edge_key`** × **`uplink_latest`** colored by **`severity`**. (2) **Timeline** — **`bfd_state_latest`** transitions. (3) **Single value** — count of **`bfd_neighbor_not_up`**. (4) **Table** — **`fault_class`**, **`peer_latest`**, **`tier0_latest`**, **`audit_actor`**. (5) **Choropleth** overlay (optional) when **`tier0_latest`** tags map to **site** codes.

## Known False Positives

**bfd_hold_gr_convergence** — **BGP** **graceful-restart** helpers deliberately hold **BFD** sessions in **INIT** while **RIB** tables rehydrate after **control-plane** hits; **`bfd_state_latest`** reads ugly even though **forwarding** planes stayed **up** on the **surviving** **edge**. Anchor suppressions to **CRQ** rows that cite **GR** maintenance and cross-check **NSX** UI **BFD** timers against **carrier** change tickets before accusing **hardware**.

**lacp_sync_renegotiation_noise** — **LACP** **PDU** timing during **ToR** upgrades raises **`uplink_not_healthy`** flaps for **seconds** while **ASIC** buffers reprogram; **Splunk** sees **DOWN**/**DEGRADED** labels without sustained **packet** **loss** once **bundles** settle. Require **two** consecutive **polls** failing across **both** **edge** nodes in the **cluster** before paging **WAN** crews, and tie noise to **`cisco:aci:syslog`** or **switch** **maintenance** bridges when **4022** feeds exist.

**inter_site_controller_degraded_poll** — **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/inter-site/status`** occasionally returns **stale** JSON when **NSX** **Manager** **VIP** members roll during **patching**; **`edge_status`** sourcetype duplicates **half**-filled **uplink** maps until the **cursor** catches up. Compare **Manager** **CLI** **`get bfd neighbors`** output and **policy** **realization** badges; suppress on **Manager** **cluster** health alarms.

**edge_service_restart_blip** — **VMware** **automatic** **service** restarts on **Edge** **VMs** after **kernel** **upgrades** restart **datapath** **proxies**; **BFD** sessions re-form within **one** **poll** interval yet **`bfd_neighbor_not_up`** rows appear briefly. Join **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** **patch** baselines and **`vmware:nsx:audit`** **edge** **upgrade** operations before opening **Sev-1** bridges.

**api_cursor_partial_page** — **REST** workers paging **BFD** neighbors sometimes emit **only** the **first** **twenty** peers; **`peer_latest`** looks **null** for **lower-priority** **VRF** legs even though **CLI** lists them—fix collectors to follow **`result_count`**/**`cursor`** fields rather than **alerting** on **empty** **facets**.

**planned_bgp_neighbor_shut** — **Change** windows that **administratively** shut **underlay** **interfaces** to **carriers** during **migrations** drive **BFD** **DOWN** intentionally—**Splunk** is correct technically but wrong **operationally** without **`nsx_planned_edge_window.csv`** lookups keyed by **`tier0_key`**.

**single_attached_uplink_test_mode** — **Labs** leave **one** **PNIC** **active** by design while **burning** **in** **firewalls**; **`uplink_fault`** stays **true** for **hours**. **Environment** **tags** on **`index`** or **`edge_cluster`** metadata should drop **non-production** **clusters** from **paging** **macros** so **engineering** time funds **true** **dual**-**hommed** **outages** only.

## References

- [VMware NSX 4.2 Routing — BFD](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/administration-guide/about-routing-in-nsx.html)
- [VMware NSX 4.2 API Reference — logical router BFD neighbors](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/api-reference.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX-T (Splunkbase 4856)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware (Splunkbase 1810)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)
- [Splunk Common Information Model — Network Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/NetworkTraffic)
